channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: You should be holding at least 1 BTC. — 89 replies — 170.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
You should be holding at least 1 BTC.

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:11 UTC

OP

Imagine not owning at least 1 BTC before institutions and governments vacuum the remaining supply while you’re still debating if Bitcoin is a scam… or just trading crypto without considering BTC long term.

Early replies (first 3)

62219945
>>62219942
Or I can trade alts (leveraged BTC) and actually make it instead of getting a 2x pump

62219995
Fuck. I have 2BTC . Is that enough bro?

62220027
>>62219995
no, i have 3. and it's not enough, we're just poor faggots

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62229578
>>62229570
Be loyal ser and start a good family

62230004
I'm all in StrategyInc tho

62230021
>>62219942
What's supposed to be happening in the op pic

62231066
>>62225044
I got jewed of my own dip, gf was home all day yesterday and i didn't buy.
I hate women so much is unbelievable

62231072
>>62229557
Why though anon? It’s not like this is some random vaporware project. They already have partnerships with names like GoMining, Ledger, a16zcrypto and others around the ecosystem.

62231081
>>62230004
Why all in? : (

62231090
>>62229433
Good advice so far.

62232211
>>62222594
There's solution for this

62233500
>>62231072
is that legit?

62233508
>>62231066
You hate woman because you like man. Gayish bitcoiner

62233709
>>62233500
Yeah anon. There’s already almost 51k BTC staked in the protocol too, which is like $4B+ worth of BTC already participating around the ecosystem.

62233716
>>62223021
You can always keep slowly adding over time anon. Most people don’t even have close to 1 BTC.

62233736
>>62224655
it will be your opportunity as well.

62233806
>>62229570
I'm from Spain and I live well. Got small biz + doing crypto + forex + stocks. Life is good.

62233821
>>62233709
never though there's protocol exists like this

62233827
>buy btc
buy link
buy ICP
buy avax

62233835
>>62228833
And Ethereum is the godess

62233840
>>62228631
Hes just a homosexual and fears for the virginity of his anus

62233880
>>62233827

worst trash available
>UNGMI

62233986
>>62233821
Yeah anon, it already exists but I think a lot of people just still aren’t paying attention to the newer BTCFi infra yet. And it’s still pretty early too since the TBVs haven’t even launched yet, which should unlock even more native BTC use cases.

62233993
>>62225388
See this anon?

62233997
>>62224679
The funniest part is you probably think debasing fiat printed out of thin air is somehow more “real” than a globally traded asset with trillions in liquidity.

62234000
>>62230021
Gonna be dead broke without BTC lol.

62234021
>>62233806
Aren't you fucked with taxes and DAC8 now, I cannot buy anymore via a cex here

62234099
>>62233840
It sucks being gay

62234109
>>62234021
do the alternative. Privacy

62234248
>>62234021
Government is shit.

62234899
>>62233986
I don’t think that’s enough when ETH literally lives and dies by DeFi activity.

62235825
>>62222594
you don't need to

62237090
>>62220146
give me money

item.title: Australia is fucked — 38 replies — 92.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Australia is fucked

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:18 UTC

OP

>CGT discount - DENIED
>Negative Gearing - DENIED
Where were you when the Australian dream died?

Early replies (first 3)

62224090
have the liberals said they will reverse it? do we just hold and wait?

62224122
>>62223850
Doesn’t this come into effect in 2027? Should I sell all my crypto before 2027?

62224148
>>62223850
its tiring living here
the new rules favour low return long duration assets. high growth assets like btc and leveraged etfs get smoked compared old rules. run a few tests... under 12 months nothing changes.
i wonder if it actually encourages speculation. might as well punt on options or asx bets miners or U.S letfs... you need BIG wins now

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224346
>>62224288
Shalom!

62224365
Relax, the changes only apply to new purchases, as long as you already have your properties you'll be paying less tax than future property owners.

62224398
>>62224346
>doesn't even reach half a billion
LOL

62224409
CGT changes for all asset types is retarded and makes it so property is still the best investment
My cope is that negative gearing changes will result in cheaper properties but higher yields as rental supply drops

62224440
>>62224365
dont care about properties, i want to know if shares are grandfathered in

62224467
>>62223850
SOMEONE EXPLAIN THIS TO ME IN ENGLISH
JUST HOW COOKED ARE WE CUNTS???

62224496
>>62224331
Right thanks m8.

62224499
>>62224440
This, except crypto and boomer rocks.

62224536
>>62224467
Return to pre-1999 CGT system.

62224566
>>62224536
CGT is theft, Fuck you albo.

62224682
>>62223850
I voted for this.

62224702
>>62224536
but... w why they do this do this to us

62224712
I'm of two minds on one hand I have about 600k AUD of cgt obligations which if I were to use the discount while I can would have to lock in maybe 100k of tax this coming year (yes I'll be at 45% bracket because I'm not a brokie)

On the other hand I'm priced out of the property market (not really but I don't want to pay 900k for a shitty Sydney apartment) that any fucking over of property investors is actually a good thing (for me and people my age)

62224719
>>62224712
Doesnt making $600k in profit automatically put you in the highest tax bracket owed regardless of how much you earned from waging?

62226023
Honestly, everyone should be sticking their money into classic cars, right this instant.

Buy a final edition V8 Landcruiser 70 series, a MK V Supra or a Y62 Patrol Warrior and just sit on it.

Watch this space, its exempt from CGT and may sky rocket in value, especially as we push forward into cucked out 4cyl and 3cyl hybrid cuckboxes.

My cars will now become my investments, fuck the Government.

62229029
>>62226023
retro games for me. if you spend less than 500 dollars on a collectible item it's tax-exempt when you sell it. they have more universal appeal than pokemon cards and many well known games are becoming extremely rare to find in complete condition.

62230860
>>62223850
>>62224346
joke of a fucking country
i wanna get out of here where to lads

62233502
>>62226023
yeah you should definitely "invest" in depreciating "assets" because Labor took advantage of their huge political capital
property is STILL the best asset class in Australia by far

62233578
>>62224702
>official inflation is 3%
>inflation is actually 5%
>your asset goes up 5%
>"actually you made a 2% profit goy, pay tax on it"

It's free money for the government in an inflationary environment which constantly understates actual inflation. Basically every asset will have taxable profits while everybody actually gets poorer.

62233595
>>62230860
Fucked if i know cunt. Stealing you pic too.
>>62233502
I'm buying gold n silver.

62233618
>>62223850
Feeling glad that I moved to the US desu

62233642
>>62233595
CGT change still applies + no passive income + risk(ier)
Rent is going to go up and existing properties may get cheaper as capital moves to new builds, which will only be temporary before investors realise no amount of tax incentives will make new builds a good investment
All that being said, I think Labor loses the election after next (which they'll win) and some of these changes will get reversed

62233646
>>62233642
>expecting liberals to do anything useful ever
lol

62233658
>>62233646
If there's one thing they'll do it's change the CGT tax because it's a layup
Also no one is mentioning that Labor blew past their immigration target for the 3rd year in a row, we have at least 6 more years of infinity Indians pumping up rents

62233662
>>62223850
>>62224281
It’s actually worse than that.
No negative gearing for new buyers, but all the existing owners still get their negative gearing grandfathered in lol.

62233868
>>62224712
You'll get the same discount regardless of when you sell. The new system only applies for gains accrued after 1 July 2027. Which also means that for anything that you sell in the future you'll need to know the price on exactly that date, which will be "lots of fun" for illiquid assets like real estate and artwork.

Personally, if LINK seems to be heading upwards by then, I'll pay CGT on the existing amount and move to Singapore for a while.

>>62226023
My last car (Falcon G6E Turbo) beat the stock market. The poor kid who bought it put himself into ridiculous debt for it.

62234274
>>62233642
All i know is i own a house and investing in non pixelated assets for retirement is still a better option than leaving money in the bank.

62234315
>>62223850
I voted for this

62234422
>>62230860
To where? Everywhere is fucked.

t. Bong

62237110
>>62224122
I thought the discount only affected real estate investments? Does it include any investments including crypto?

From what I've heard it isn't happening until 2027. Gives me time to renovate and sell one of my apartments.

item.title: I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. — 15 replies — 60.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS.

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:41 UTC

OP

I'm 33 and still working an entry level job as a BDR in tech/SaaS. What the fuck do i do now? I don't even like what I do

Early replies (first 3)

62236318
>enter thread
>download op image
>leave thread

62236322
>>62236309
Sex!

62236326
>>62236309
I deserve to have sex with women who look like this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236330
>>62236309
At least you’re not a 30 year old entry level analyst dealing with retarded ass sales reps all day long. I bet you get paid better too.

62236409
>>62236309
That is a job for someone literally a decade younger than you. Why not just get an AE job?

62236420
>>62236409
I might have at this point. My current employer is Gate keeping me from getting an AE promotion. I've applied so many times for remote AE positions , but they won't hire you unless you have previous AE experience. I might have to start applying to on site roles now which will suck, but at least if they accept me then I'll have some experience

62236517
>>62236309
I'm 33 and work in Walmart as a stocker

62236592
>>62236420
It's cuz you're (literally) competing against 500-1000+ applicants per role, even including the on-site roles. And no, baseddit strategies like
> just go to the last page on linkedin bro, less applicants to compete against that way bro
> you can't give up bro! it's a numbers game bro!
will not work because everyone else out there is already using the same strategy, hence the flood of applicants per job which make it impossible to even get an interview. And for that linkedin point, used to work but not anymore because every job posted on there has the stupid "promoted" tag which exposes it to the entire globe of applicants within seconds. Each job posting gets bot applicant flooded within micro seconds. Many of these applicants also have rockstar-tier resumes too so good luck competing against them.

tldr; job market is PERMANENTLY fucked, thank AI and globalism for this mess

62236645
>>62236309
>33
>entry level
nigga you lost a whole decade of your life you need to figure out what happened there

62236684
>>62236318
kek

62236700
>>62236517
How long have you been working there? Was it easy to get the job?

62236733
>>62236517
Become a stonker.

62237056
>>62236309
just get promoted to AE like the rest of the sales monkeys. its not any harder. literally apply yourself to applying for better roles the way you prospect for opportunities in your day job u sped

62237118
>>62236318
literally me

62237179
>>62237118
It's some kind of long format ai clip I found but didn't save in time
Shit is hot af

item.title: TICK TOCK WAGIE — 12 replies — 48.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
TICK TOCK WAGIE

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:33 UTC

OP

THOSE 48 HOURS WILL PASS IN A FLASH

Early replies (first 3)

62236431
>>62236166
Jannies, why do you allow these shit threads every damn week at the same time

62236451
>>62236431
continue reporting them

62236469
>>62236166
kek

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236486
>>62236431
The janny already works too hard!

62236491
>>62236166
Why does everyone think wagies work on weekdays only?
Don't wagies work weekends too?

62236495
>>62236431
>Jannies, why do you allow these shit threads every damn week at the same time

62236534
>>62236431
>>62236451
Cope, wagie

62236570
I'm making 5k a month to jumpstart my investments and savings account. Get bent.

62237115
I CANT WAIT FOR MONDAY MORNING

62237130
>>62236166

I shitpost for hours on other boards. I make funny slightly unrelated comments on /biz/ and I get insta banned or put in the cuck cage for a day.

fuck niggers, fuck jews

And most importantly FUCK JANNIES

62237133
op looks forward all week to making this thread

62237155
>>62237133
5 days of neeting alone, you wouldn't deprived him of this affordable pleasure would you?

item.title: remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022 — 11 replies — 44.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
remember when LINK cucks spent the entirety of 2022

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:28 UTC

OP

blaming Bulgarians and NEXO for their shitty price performance while calling anyone who made fun of their bags Bulgarians

Early replies (first 3)

62236293
>>62236269
kek fuddie there was literal proof

62236306
>>62236293
are Bulgarians the reason you're still a poor baggie in 2026? lol

62236412
>>62236269
LOL

i remember repeatedly being called bulgarian back then

then when that wore off there was the guy who always posted the na pidgeon language or whatever implying i was african or something. never really understood that one

and on and on it goes... the cult always has a mythological bad guy who seemingly has so much power he can suppress their billion dollar asset indefinitely

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236572
>>62236412
hahahahahaha I remember that too. There was a schizo Linkie calling people Nigerians. The cherry on top of the cake is that Chainlink panders to niggers and even pays them to participate in crypto conferences

62236597
It's always one more integration until moon.
One more partnership until moon.
One more law passed until moon.
The latest carrot on a stick is the last one. For real this time.
This year is the year. All the breadcrumbs are fulfilled. Or something. Any pattern recognition is just "fud."

62236602
>>62236293
No one needs to fud LINK, sweatie.

62236652
>>62236269
Why even make fun of it anymore? This project has gone nowhere for almost 5 years maybe longer, at this point you're giving this dead project more attention than it deserves

62237093
>>62236652
OP and everyone ITT holds LINK

62237126
>>62237093
Baggie cannot envision anyone not holding LINK (also can't rotate an apple in his head or tell you how he would feel if he'd skipped breakfast this morning)

62237134
i remember being called a bulgarian and it was crazy funny because im actually a bulgarian living in bulgaria

62237144
>>62236269
Cripple

item.title: Bitcoin "wastes energy." XRP has declared war on real cryptocurrency! — 13 replies — 33.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin "wastes energy." XRP has declared war on real cryptocurrency!

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:20 UTC

OP

When did this happen?? I thought Ripple banking partners were just full of shit, liars, and purposely deceitful!

They are now saying real consensus mechanisms are "wasting energy!" XRP has 130 validators, VETTED BY THEM, WITH BULLSHIT CONSENSUS SOFTWARE.

Early replies (first 3)

62231653
> we have incentive to do this thing
> totally btc is bad because of le bad energy use!!

62231656
>>62231647

KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62231682
I pray something awful happens to this company

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231978
>>62231647
but are they wrong doe??

62232008
>>62231978

Yes

62232419
>>62231647
>They are now saying real consensus mechanisms are "wasting energy!" XRP has 130 validators, VETTED BY THEM, WITH BULLSHIT CONSENSUS SOFTWARE

6% up btw
Touch grass fudcuck

62232757
>>62231647
This isn't a bad thing.
Bitcoin is more useful than the whole country of Argentina.
Maybe we should cut power to Argentina since they don't produce much of a value.

62232870
>>62231647
Kaboom

62233780
>>62231647
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62236240
>>62231647
KABOOOOOM

62236298
>>62232757

62237114
>>62231647

62237117

also I tried to go to both last year but I was denied going to swell
con just let me paypiggy my way in

item.title: >dude you need a house with lots of LAND >spends all of his free time mowing his huge lawn — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>dude you need a house with lots of LAND >spends all of his free time mowing his huge lawn

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:38 UTC

OP

>dude you need a house with lots of LAND
>spends all of his free time mowing his huge lawn

Early replies (first 3)

62237087
>>62237073
That's slave behavior. Why not make a food forest out of it as long as it's in the countryside? Huge berry bushes, fruits trees, hazelnut, some big trees inbetween and if you like chucked or a pig.

62237094
Yeah it seems like its only worth it if you are actually loaded and can pay some country bumpkin $40k a year to manage the land for you

62237096
>>62237087
>why not make something which requires even more regular slave behavior than mowing the lawn

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62237103
you can just mow a small area where you and your family can relax, its actually very unhealthy to mow your land constantly

62237125
what if you had a robot lawnmower and you could mow your lawn via smartphone app

62237127
if you have a big land you can organize some cute places where your wife could be stretched by BBC's without your kids knowing it

62237168
>>62237096
Then don't have chickens or a pig. Berry bushes and fruit trees or nut trees require only minimal work in spring.

item.title: Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit? — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit?

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:36 UTC

OP

Are lots of people(im speaking USA for me) just retarded with money and dont/cant admit? I get kids, hard times, job loss, emergencies, etc… but solo living doesnt seem anywhere as hard as people make it out to be. Dual income with someone who also is frugal seems like a bonus.

Im talking in the vein of saving money. Im excluding the housing market issue and job market for those in specific careers where growth is fucked atm.

Purely to get by, in a safe place, isnt very hard, Especially if solo. I hear co-workers complain but refuse roommates, they get new cars too soon instead of functional cheap used, they got subscriptions out the ass, they fucking travel and stack shit on CC, and buy useless shit. I feel like even bringing this up is the equivalent of just flat out saying.

Like my states COL has gone up 100-150% across the board. I have to move but see it as a saving opportunity. support networks generally dissolve heavily for most in 30s to 40s unless you are insanely fortunate. Marriages, kids, sick relatives, yes these are reasons to stay for some but im talking about those where that isnt the concern, where just staying for known “comfort” isnt worth it long term.

Its also not just my own life im talking about, people really dont seem to want to sacrifice anything when even a little goes a long way. Food is the most basic but fucking insane one to me. How is a single person spending $100 a week on food? Mines like $80 for 2 weeks and I can go even cheaper, in a HCOL(scaled to wages, the cost went up, not wages, only those on the property ladder benefitted)


Am I ignorant?

Early replies (first 3)

62236887
>>62236793
my wife spends $300 a week on food. I tell her I'd be eating ramen in our current situation if she weren't around.

62236933
>>62236793
>$100 week on food
Honestly my man, that’s literally the bare minimum you can spend. I’m assuming you’re ignorant/blind to the poison we’re being fed.

62236974
>>62236933
I cook everything and wait for sales. I got 24 good eggs for $8. Bake own bread, 2 loafs, like $4 no poison. Bundle of bananas weekly, like 5$. 2 weeks of breakfast, $19

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62237102
>>62236974
Life is for living, not just surviving

62237153
>>62237102
But im saving shitloads so I can have better living later. Its why I saved so much prior because I had tons of medical shit for 5 years and a few ER visits. I would have medical debt. Instead im not and making a move.


My friends dad sacraficed a lot so he could help my friend get her first house and all he does is travel right now.

62237162
>>62236793
>How is a single person spending $100 a week on food?
Retards who earn $12/hour will buy a $25 doordash lunch and not realize they just gave away 2.5 hours of their life (after taxes) for a lunch they should have skipped because they are obese.

item.title: The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History — 81 replies — 29.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The BIGGEST Crypto Integration in History

Open thread • Replies: 81 • Updated: 2026-05-15 18:55 UTC

OP

Historic moment as Chainlink services have been selected to be used by the DTCC! This is monumental to the crypto and blockchain industry. They even pinned the tweet! Wow, to think how far we have come as an industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62224616
Is this good or bad?

62224627
when are people going to wake up and realize that chainlink is the only crypto that matters? when will the fucking price finally go up?

62224642
>>62224627
I think you honestly really needed the spoon feeding from autists here back in 2017 to gather the confidence we have in Link. By the time Link got on the crypto map in 2020 or whenever people were cemented in their cliques for whatever coin they believed in. Most of the crypto space are normalfaggots. I’ve gotten a handful of people to buy, but you can never tell them to go all in cuz they are not emotionally invested in link. I got6 people to invest 300, 300, 700, and 500 and 400 and 1700. Almost all of them

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62227473
>>62224616
bad, sell the news event + token not needed
>>62224627
chainlink might matter, LINK does not

>>62224974
many ICO bros made it back in 2020 at $50 withtheir 50-70k stacks, sure they're no warrent buffets but it was enough to live comfortably, too bad most didn't sell

>>62225125
yeah, as long as the tokens aren't needed you can have top of the NASDAQ sucking Chainlinks dick, people just stopped caring, it's not 2017 anymore. Back then it would've sent LINk to triple digits, but those days are long gone, peoeple wanna see actual demand nowadays, but it ain't coming, because Chainlink /= LINK

62227498
>>62224863
Sui has always been 1k

62227508
>>62227498
jesus dude that's like $10k, even if LINK does a 3x (and that's being generous) you'd only have $30k, what are you gonna do with that? lol

62227538
SERGEY BETRAYS US

SELL SELL FOR YOUR LIVES

62227556
>>62224958
This anon is correct.

62227563
>>62227508
Link will be 1k+

62227593
>>62227563
No it will pump to $30 and dump back down to $5.

62229154
>>62224577
it's big news!

62229156
>>62229154

62229158
>>62229156

62229164
>>62229158
Is that fat bitch* still fudding?

*the woman not Sergey

62229165
>>62229164
She's Nadine Chakar from the DTCC and she's partners with Chainlink

62232247
Bump. They trying to slide the other thread so hard lol.

62232345
>>62227563
lmao
lmao
lmao
lmao

62232462
>>62232345
Swift, DTCC, Fidelity plus the other 600 more.

62232545
>>62224577
how tf did we get to $50 on nothing but with literally everything we are barely afloat at $10?

62232606
>>62232545
because when it hit $50 everything else in crypto was pumping for no reason at all, leading to a terrible crash, and the market has basically been dead for 6 years, with only bitcoin seeing consistent gains and solana's shitcoin casino being lamer and scammier than eth's. When accounting for inflation, this entire market has been on life support since 2021.

62232658
I'm so excited for you crazy kids

62232694
>DTCC settles $2.5 quadrillion per year
>Chainlink gets used for 1% of that
>1% of the settled value as collateral
>Full dilution
>1 LINK = $250

62232746
>>62232545
Did you miss Ari-Sama’s thread earlier this year? It’s being shorted

62232794
>>62232545
the fat fuck scammer has released like 400 million coins onto the market since then, diluting your bags. You got scammed.

62232796
>>62226079
I’m 45 next week

62232803
>>62232694
You factor swift and every single other company ever and plus front running and suddenly the meme $81,000 number doesn’t seem very meme.

62232805
>>62227397
Chainlink gatekeeps itself. I’ve told countless normies about a link in 8 years and about 6 people have bought in. And all but 1 have less than 1,000 and the other has 2,000. I also told some kid when playing poker and he got like half his frat to buy in. The people who I have told who didn’t buy wouldn’t buy anyway.

And anyone who does buy isn’t gonna buy anything more than a sui stack at most because you have to be emotionally invested in any investment in order to buy a lot. I have a very affable personality and energy and even tho I’m totally dumb on anything technical…. People believe me. Oh I also got a chick to buy 3,000 link during covid who has a husband.

62234063
>>62232794
Which is less than most.
Bitcoin for instance released half its supply by 2013-2014, which is also when it pumped the most.

In fact, all cryptos in general pumped the hardest when they inflated the hardest.

62234092
Chainlink is literally a cuckold club full of incels waiting for their "real life to begin". They've been bagholding for 9 years while Sergey (their God) dumped on them.

THE cuckolds of crypto

62234112
>>62224577
No greater fools left i see.

62236050
>>62232694
>MOAC
please give me what ever copium you are taking

62236496
duuuuuude don't question the cult
duuuuuuude this investment is for geniuses only
duuuuuuude partnerships


>Despite its internal culture of obfuscation, Enron projected an illusion of transparency, largely through a sophisticated public relations strategy. The company's charismatic leaders, Ken Lay and Jeff Skilling, were often seen on financial networks and at industry conferences, extolling Enron's virtues and its role as a market innovator.

>The company's annual reports and press releases were masterclasses in euphemism and grandiosity. Technical jargon and ambiguous language created an aura of complexity that dissuaded scrutiny. Questions were deflected with a common refrain: Enron was too innovative to be understood by the uninitiated.

62236514
>>62232694
>duuuuuude if we just capture 1%

you need to watch this https://www.youtube.com/shorts/7Z4-WRmZ8j8

Kraken has moved to CCIP.

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-15 19:24 UTC

OP

62230910
>>62230891

62230914
WAGMI WAGMI

62230921
>shovel salesman announces that shovels are useful, actually

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62231801
>>62231282
https://x.com/LayerZero_Core/status/1996618258066411820

5 months ago.

62231802
i think it might actually be LINK's time

62231853
>>62230891
>>62231282

Lido too now lol
Fresh off the presses

62231868
>>62231843
It was foretold. Obvious in hindsight.

62231901
cuckolds are embarassing lmao

price + chart

62231946
>>62231853
ALL AT ONCE

62231990
>>62231853
oooooooof

62231992
>>62231946
OLD NEWS buddy

62232282
>>62230891

This is actually FUD. Cross Chain Interoperability Protocol is a load of gobbly gook (I skimmed thoroughly) and chainlink still hasn't proven its original use case (solving the problem of getting data into smart contracts in a decentralized manner).

The very concept of Sergey's oracle system is unproven, although sensible. All this bullshit being put on top is not "exciting" or "value-added," it is FUD. It is a stream of bullshit without any actual real world uses.

You retards are getting excited by someone scooping shit on top of technological marvels. Imagine the first airplane, thats a blockchain. Now imagine a woman jumping on top the cockpit and taking a huge dump. Thats what your getting excited about.

62232408
>>62231646
Saar I'm sooo white saar!

62232418
Wait I thought LINK was a SCAM?!? You're telling me the most upstanding, honest, and forthcoming company in crypto, King Kraken, has literally entrusted their reputation in the hands of a fat russian big mac enjoyer? So LINK is not a scam? Somebody comfort me please...

62232437
>>62232418
the scoundrel sergey is very convincing

62232441
>>62230891
Thats cool and all but.... one small problem.... uhh... price??? Oh...... and... chart??

62232461
>>62232408
that's adem newfaggot
lurk moar

62232525
>>62232282
This so much, all the top financial institutions of the world are getting absolutely gobblecocked by sergey

Now X R P. THAT is the one correct thing they are choosing!!

62236073
>>62231115
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62236163
>>62231853
This is old news isn't it? Lido was already with CCIP. They're just now publishing their rationale behind the decision.

62236196
>>62236073
doing great thanks
oh btw: pee plus see, bitch

62236449
>>62231646

yeah for a guy that's "winning" you don't seem very happy about that

>>62231646

you're addicted to keeping track of a random online personality jacking off
dude that's fucking wild

have you told anyone irl?

62236468
>>62236449
Hope you're doing well, Adem

62236472
hey guys --- how's the new "integration" going today? lets check the numbers shall we?


>CCIP fees last 24h: $637

bahahahaha

>Link price action last 24h: -5.2%

bahahahahahahahaah holy shit


nobody, and i mean NOBODY, gets viciously c u c k h o l d e d quite like the boyscouts.... and then comes back time and time again, absolutely BEGGING for more

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62236479
>>62230910
Oh my fuckk you weren't even lying.

I hate this gay ass market.

62236498
>>62231117
You know he probably owns more Link than almost anyone else here, hes just be a dickhole for fun because he knows whats coming.

62236508
>>62230891
i saw kraken now allows margin gambling without having to have a huge portfolio. pretty cool imo. as far as link, i don't think about it at all

62236594
>>62236163
Well here's a completely new one.

62236605
OMFG WE ARE MOONING!!!!!

62236616
HOLY FUCKING SHIT IT JUST KEEPS GOING!!!!!

62236626
>faggot ass stinkies gloating over nothing
This thread is now the $9 dollar waiting room

62236630

they will be forced to admit that poverty is their only bedfellow

item.title: I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in t… — 6 replies — 23.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in t…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:28 UTC

OP

I read this article a while back, it said the Microsoft employs more millionaire secretaries than any other company in the world. they chose stock options over Christmas bonuses. It was a good move. I remember there was this photo of one of the groundskeepers standing next to his Ferrari. You see shit lke this, and you just can't see. Makes you think its possible even easy. And you turn on the TV and there's just more of it. The 87 million dollar lottery winner. That kid actor who just made 20 million on his last movie. The Internet stock that shot through the roof. You could have made millions off it if you just got in early. And that's exactly what I wanted to do. Get in. I didn't want to be an innovator. I just wanted to make the quick, easy buck. I just wanted in. The Notorious BIG put it best. "Your either slingin crack rock, or you got a wicked jump shot." Nobody wants to work for it anymore. There's no honor in taking that after-school job at Mickey-Dee's. The honor's in the dollar kid. So in the white boy way of slingin crack rock, I became a stockbroker.

Early replies (first 3)

62232431
N

62233749
I

62236821
G

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62236854
G

62236955
o

62237142
>>62232420
>I remember there was this photo of one of the groundskeepers standing next to his Ferrari

poor people love to squander the first chance their entire lineage has

item.title: >never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak… — 64 replies — 23.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>never had a gf >never been on a date >now 36 >had jobs >go to gym >finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25 >mak…

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-05-15 18:57 UTC

OP

>never had a gf
>never been on a date
>now 36
>had jobs
>go to gym
>finally lose weight and have a BMI less than 25
>make multiple dating profiles on different apps
>zero matches

So I'm a confirmed subhuman. And, judging by the responses to ugly males who post their pics on Reddit and ask why they get no matches, normies openly laugh at me and think I deserve a low status life of slavery.

Why should I give a fuck about putting in effort at work or saving the west or whatever? Any sort of participation is a scam for someone like me.

Early replies (first 3)

62223666
>>62223664
>>>/adv/

62223769
>>62223664
depends how tall u are and if u have any real ugliness causing things like deformity or burn scars etc.

U probably are just average looking and if u arent 5'4'' u will find a gf

62224072
>>62223664
are you swiping on fat women or only the stacies?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62233004
>>62231869
no shit dumbass. if youve had meaningless sex with 50 women you've fried your brain romantically

62233012
kill fat people

62233014
bro online dating is HORRIBLE. Unless you're like the top 5% of hot guys out there it's gonna be a depressing humiliation ritual. You prob have to hire a professional photographer and make it look like you have a vibrant, exciting life with a bunch of staged pics. I used shitty selfies a decade ago, but now it doesnt cut it.

62233017
>>62233014
this. If not a chad you need to be fully optimized with pics and lifestyle. Otherwise irl, which probably means 1 girl opportunity every few months otherwise you spoil the well

62233024
>>62232652
This
It’s sickening once you look into it.
Women at large are getting blamed when half the women on there are fake profiles anyway. There’s plenty of fake men’s profiles now too, dedicated to romance scams. Which is a whole other issue, men and women being victims

62233040
>>62233017
>that ugly guy with super polished tryhard profile

this unironically gives girls the ick

62233064
>>62233040
they get the ick from the guy being ugly. Selecting photos where he looks most attractive is only going to help compared to bathroom selfies. If he does that he'll get 0 matches guaranteed.

62233065
>>62233040
If hes ugly they already have the ick

62233066
>>62233065
exactly. The most important tip is don't be ugly. 2nd is don't be autistic as fuck. 3rd actually talk to girls whether online or irl whatever.

62233115
>>62223664
Dating apps arent for dating they're for sluts to get their holes blown out by a small selection of chads and fuckbois.

62233200
>>62233115
>chads
>fuckbois
what's the difference?

62233241
>>62233200
Chads don't have to try because their appearance does everything. Fuckbois have to try hard to get women to fuck them. They still have fuck but the level of effort is completely different.

62233271
>>62223664
I posted my face on soc once when I was drunk.

Everyone gave me an 7 or 8/10. I still feel ugly though.

62233283
>>62231723
5/10 roasties only swipe on 8+

62233302
>>62233283
/thread

All women swipe 3+ points up. If you're only attracting morbidly obese women, down syndrome women and old fat single mothers, you're an above average looking guy.

These studies have already been done 1,000 times. Average looking guys get 1 match a month abs it's usually a fake/bot account or a 300+ pound clown with 20 fishhooks in its face.

62233310
>>62223664
your ugly ancestors got pussy somehow and so can you

62233355
>>62223664
Remember, for every beautiful person, there must be 5 ugly ones.

I don't make the rules.

62233408
>>62223664
Why do you want a girlfriend? If you want to LOVE a woman you are gay, sorry to tell you. You need to start niggermaxxing, learn to pump and dump

62233456
>>62223664
Go to Philippines, Moldova, or Gambia, depending on what race you prefer. You'll get more girlfriends than you know what to do with.

I'd post some pix of hot bare-titties Himba girls but the jannies get all assblasted about that for some reason.

62233560
>>62232980
>Where in person did you meet your wife?
GF of a friend introduced me to her.
This strategy requires having friends, I know that's too much to ask of GenZ nowadays.

62233715
>>62233271
i never posted on soc but i did on the site photofeeler where people rate your pic. Out of 120 rates i averaged to a 3/10

which makes sense since im khv at 30. I am way too scared to post my face on this site though thats crazy

62233733
you can't be this naive at 36, its surprising how many manchildren there are in the west still chasing dating and love and marriage when your zoomer generation have figured it out for the scam that it is

62233792
>>62233733
yep, one of the most popular streamers smashes his face with a hammer... lmfao

all of this was figured out over a decade ago but no one wanted to listen.

62234041
>>62232735
>social pariah wonders why he's not surrounded by a harem of qt3.14s
many such cases

62234043
>>62233715
I'm 32 and a virgin. Just dick in pussy/ass virgin. I've done everything else. Also 6'3" and slender/fit.

62234052
Maybe you shouldn't spend so much time around permavirgins (reddit)

62234972
>>62233241
oh, I guess I'm a fuckboi. I've optimized everything possible and fuck a lot. I also live in a small town and have chicks drive 70 miles to my place on the regular for a first meetup. Got one tonight and another next Thurs for a "sleepover"

>>62233283
They do but eventually they run out of those dudes or they don't followup with meeting up. Sub8s wont see the chicks who just signed up for the apps but they'll likely see her after a few months. She'll humble herself after these dudes just pump and dump them over and over and they start going for the charming less attractive guy who ultimately just fucks and dumps then anyway, then they go for true beta bucks after that.

>>62233310
there werent many options back then and girls needed a guy for survival. Now every girl has the world at her fingertips and their own money.

>>62233456
yea this is the only option for a sub5 guy or mgtow

>>62233560
Yea your way is the best. I had a few of those situation but I never liked the girl enough or she had to move for college etc unfortunately. It really comes down to luck.

>>62233733
>chasing dating and love
this was exciting when I was a teenager. As an adult it's only fun to pump and dump. I do think love can exist if you find a good woman to build a family with though.

62235106
>>62223664
Bro stop using apps. Also those things you listed are no substitute for being able to competently speak to and entertain women. It is, unfortunately, a learned skill. You have to practice. A good way to learn is to flirt with women a little older than you. They typically like the attention from a younger man and are more forgiving of missteps.

62235448
Being ugly doesn't matter. Being 36 doesn't matter. Focus on confidence, charisma and making women feel heard and seen. Don't put so much weight on it, be a fun person to be around. Play around and test things out. You do really make your own reality. Don't convince yourself of these negative things.

62236519
>>62223664
>1pbtid demoralization
>>/glownigger/

U.S. STOCK MARKET SOARS

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-15 21:03 UTC

OP

After XI endorsed MAGA in his speech

Omg

Early replies (first 3)

62231396
omg number go up!!11

62231594
>>62231396
Based

62235310
>>62231396
Based

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62235322
That didnt age well

62235357
>>62231391
>2% up
>"Soars"

62235854
>>62231391
>77T valuation

>loses 5%

>3 TRILLION WIPED FROM THE MARKET!!!

62235883
>>62231391
Wat? It's dumping. Along with crypto no less

62235927
>>62231396

62235933
We got too cocky

62236966
>>62231396
Based

62237071
It's all in red today because people sold the news about chyna and XI not going all in on nvidia. Basically the meeting was a nothingburger so no good news in china was "bad".

But let me tell you, nobody in the world, nobody is even close to nvidias AI tech or chips not even china. Nvidia will be a 10 trillion company by 2035.

item.title: Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio… — 44 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main questio…

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-05-15 18:24 UTC

OP

Y axis is in points on NQ. White line is combined blend, other lines are each individual strategy I use. My main question is at the bottom below the dashed line, so you can skip all of this text if you want.

Is anyone here good with statistics or math? Idk the correct field that would apply here, some math shit.
I’ve been working on a strategy for years at this point, but I’ve been focused on a single thing for about the last half year. I’ve back tested it till 2020 and its results are the photo. I wanted to use it with prop firms to make some cash since the potential profit with them is way higher than using my own cash, but the drawdowns are too severe. And prop firms are hella strict on their drawdowns.
I’ve run the math and if I scale down enough to where I can trade without blowing up my account, at the rate of withdrawals and taxes and splits, I’d be working for nothing. Min wage basically. And if I scale up then I just blow up accounts due to drawdown. I don’t care about profit either. I need smoothness. Even if I take a major hit in points, so long as it becomes smoother that’s still fine since I can just scale up on prop firms.

It’s basic metrics right now are
WR: 53.78%
Profit Factor: 1.36
Trades Per Day: 8.33

There’s a lot more data but that’s the gist.
The strategy is a blend so it’s RR varies, but it’s pretty close to 1:1RR

—————————-
Here’s my main question: the strategy currently averages 8 trades a day. That’s a lot. Surely I can cut that down to 1-2 and increase the winrate right, therefore increasing its smoothness right? Surely. I’ve tried a lot of things but I’ve failed all analytical approaches. I’ve done a lot of digging and I think I’m just out of my element here.

I think my request is gonna fall on deaf ears but I’m hopeful. Maybe someone earnest and smarter than me can help.

Early replies (first 3)

62220033
BUmp before I sleep

62220056
buy an ad grok

62222767
>>62220056
Does my post sound like an Ai made it lol? It’s a Genuine question

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62224207
>>62224200
the strategies are a mixture of VWAP and ORB. i used a base idea and iterated from there, testing different rule sets and requirements before deciding on the current setup. do you have a background in statistics or quantitative analysis?

62224225
>>62223860
holding for seconds or minutes at a time is usually a scalper. if you're holding for 30 mins to hours+ i dont think 1:1RR is efficient, there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing and achieve the same reward.

clearly you have some type of edge. you could either trade your own capital in MNQ contracts or try to make the drawdowns fit for the prop firm plan. you dont have to trade full size NQs you could have a lot more flexibility with MNQ.

>strat takes 8 damn trades a day, surely there's some way to cut the fluff down to just a few high quality ones
this is where you have to drill down to every trade taken and qualitatively sort out the lower quality trades. theres certainly nuances and details that differ across the trades taken so finding a better way to filter out the bad ones should yield better efficiency. you cant do that type of work through top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

62224267
>>62224188
I have it working, it was running live for a few weeks. Made me an average 1% per day, but upon the first sign of danger I panicked and turned it off. Working on retooling it to notify me and I can configure it on the fly.

62224277
>>62224225

>there has to be some way to cut your risk spacing

Well anon, that’s basically the core problem. I don’t know how to qualitatively filter out lower-quality trades.

I’ve tried pulling data at the exact point of entry, things like EntryFromOpenPct, EntryFromVWAPPct, Prior10RangePct, Prior30ChurnPct, DayRangeSoFarPct, ES confirmation, EMA state, signal candle body/range, stop distance, target distance, planned RR, and a few other metrics.

The idea was to see if winners or losers had anything consistent in common, then build some kind of grading system to decide before entry whether a trade was worth taking. But that ended up being a dead end too. Although I am still testing variants of that as we speak with harsher filters, I'm not super hopeful.

I also don’t want to rely entirely on ChatGPT or AI to generate ideas, because it could easily lead me astray and I wouldn’t know, since I’m out of my element here. That’s why I’m stuck.

>you don’t have to trade full-size NQs, you could have more flexibility with MNQ

I looked into that too. The issue is that if I size down enough to survive the drawdowns, my scale becomes too small. It would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.

And once I run the math, I’d be spending 3–5 hours a day trading for basically nothing. Not even $100/day in actual profit after costs and resets.

So technically, I do have a path to payouts right now. The problem is the math says it’s just not worth it.

>top down back-testing it has to be done trade by trade.

Sometimes if I have an idea, I’ll manually backtest it through a few days or weeks first. If it shows promise, then I’ll programmatically test it across the full dataset.

There are definitely some strategies that work well in specific regimes. The problem is that by the time you realize the regime has shifted, you’ve usually already given back most or all of what you made from it.

62224392
>>62224277
that sounds like the right path to be taking to find that extra quality filter. maybe you need more testing time to find that last filter piece. i've learned that more criteria is not always yielding more efficient results though, you can end up filtering out alot of good trades and reducing trade volume too much. i dont think AI is good for idea generation for trading or signals, personally i use AI to run all the math stats and simulations exclusively.

>would take weeks or months to pass evaluations, then even more time to reach payouts.
I passed most evaluations in 1 week to 8 weeks. i feel like if you're trying to pass in less than a week your size is prob too big. the 1 week pass was during a highly volatile period where i got much bigger wins.

It seems like you're on the right track. in trading, usually just doing 1 or 2 things really really well is better than having too many tools. i still disagree with your core stats of 1:1RR and requirements of high win-rate. i know theres traders that make that work but i've always wanted 1 big win to pay for 3+ losers. the big fish arent always biting but when you land 1 you realize chasing small fish all the time is a waste.

62224555
>>62224392
Everything you mentioned: sizing down, different contract sizes, different RR models, higher RR/lower win rate systems, and higher win rate/lower RR systems… I’ve already considered, tested, or looked into.

The main issue is the math. If it takes me around five weeks to pass a typical $50,000 evaluation with a $3,000 profit target, that works out to about $120 per trading day. If I then use the same strategy on a funded account, which makes sense since the rules are usually very similar, that same strategy is still only producing about $120 per day. After the prop firm split and taxes, that is well under $100 per day, which is not really viable long term or enough for financial independence.

The only way to make that work would be running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate. If the strategy hits a deeper drawdown period, I could lose all of them at once.

So the issue is that I either size down so much that I spend thousands on evals, resets, and activation fees just to withdraw a few hundred dollars later, or I need to find a strategy that can grow more aggressively.

That is why I’m focused on a higher win rate + a higher profit factor. A lower win rate strategy might still have good EV on paper, but it also means more losing streaks and more resets along the way. A higher win rate gives me a better chance to build a cushion before those rough periods come.

With that being said, I’m not going to pretend I know everything. After all, I’m the one who came to this board asking for help.

The truth is, I don’t currently have a strategy with the kind of stats you’re describing (3:1RR) The one I have now, with its current win rate and profit factor, is the best I’ve been able to build so far hence why I’ve been sticking with it.

62224557
>>62224267
1% a day means 500% returns after just half a year. If you got a strategy like that then you’d be flying in a private jet within a few years lmao

62224754
>>62224555
>running multiple accounts, but that creates the same problem, just spread across many accounts all of which cost money to start and activate.
most prop firms let you have multiple accounts and if the economics are good enough for 1 you should always max the number of accounts for better scale. other prop firms just give you a bigger account (100k or 250k). yeah averaging $100 per day for 250 trading days per year is only $25k but ideally you get to 5x scale within the first 2 months then thats $125k per year. you can grind that out for years or find the next level of scale like trading for clients, another prop firm, or cut a deal with the prop firm you scaled up with.

idk if you're all in on this or its a side-job and you wage but its always about leverage past credibility & performance record to gain greater scale until you manage an uncomfortable amount of money.

62227720
>>62224754
>if the economics are good for one

Well anon, the economics AINT good enough for one hahaha. that's what I'm trying to solve. It's the question I asked in the very first post.

62228083
>>62224207
I am self taught and very well versed in quant the highest sharpe ive achieved is 1.9

62228084
>>62224557
that means hes larping and did the ting

62228111
>>62228084
It feels good to be doubted because what you've done amazes someone. Thanks for giving me the inspiration to keep at it.

62228122
>>62228083
Okay, here's my conundrum then: Suppose you have a list of 10K+ trades spanning from 2020 until now. Each trade has its data attached: date, entry, PnL, and any other metrics that might be important.

How would you go about finding some method or filter to cut out the low-quality trades from the high-quality trades? Ultimately, my strategy relies on momentum, so days where price chops heavily hurt me badly.

Perhaps I could find a way to either identify a chop day before it happens, or find a new addition or advanced criteria that would allow me to dynamically adjust my profit target and stop-loss based on what the model forecasts for that day.

Those are two ideas I have, but I haven’t been able to implement them simply because I’m not sure how to go about it.

I’ve checked the data and found that even the losing trades often have some degree of favorable movement before they eventually lose. That makes me wonder if I could find something that would tell me to reduce my profit target size for that day based on XYZ criteria.

Again, I’m kind of just spitballing because I genuinely don’t know where else to go with this. Everything I’ve tried to improve win rate by cutting losses has inevitably cut out winners as well. So I’m not sure what the right approach is.

62228424
bump

62228603
>>62228122
Low quality and high quality? That’s any MFT Strat lol

62228693
>>62228603
also if i help i get nothing from this

62228705
>>62228603
the strategy is not anything that would require low latency at least to my knowledge since the avg trade lasts anywhere from half an hour to several hours. again, i got no idea where to go from here. any tips would be appreciated

>>62228693
well if you helped me improve it, you could do whatever you want with it. It's not some proprietary strategy that requires some exotic data, it's pretty simple and generic. maybe that's why it's had a minor edge for so long. I would only be trading on it using prop firms, their sim accounts don't trade on live markets. and even if i did, i'm only trading NQ which is hella liquid. And as I've mentioned before, once I make a certain amount of money I'm done for good. Never gonna trade again. In theory if I could get this thing to a workable state, scaling it up would take a few months and I'd hit my goal within a year or two.

62232797
bump

>>62230627
Also I thought about this a bit more, edge erosion is a real thing. The ironic thing is that if I released it with the intention of helping others, some actual firm might come along and pick that up and run with it. I recall there was this one strategy I was researching a while back. It performed well from 2016 all the way until 2020. But after 2020 till now it started performing worse and worse and worse. Until 2025 and 2026 where the returns were so ass that the only way you'd profit off of it was to essentially scalp it down to the ticks. Only a group with low latency and a huge bankroll would profit off of that. I saw live how a strategy had it's edge eroded.

I'm not claiming what I have is something that amazing. but that is a worry. At the most I'd share it with close friends and family and help them succeed. After all, what is the point of wealth if not to help others.
